definition: The abstract concept of a user trigger that initiates a process or side-effect (e.g., 'Save', 'Delete'). It decouples the trigger (Button) from the handler.
usage: Standardized by Action Intent.

definition: A long-running operation that has gone async — handed off from an in-view loader past its escalation threshold to a persistent surface, where it continues while the user is free to navigate away.
usage: Standardized by Background Task Intent. The receiving end of the Loader Intent's escalation: async handoff; hosted in an app-shell region (Layout Intent dock).

definition: A cancelable event fired before a visibility change. Call preventDefault() to cancel the transition.
usage: Matches Popover API pattern. Fired by ViewEngine on all View blocks. Event detail contains oldState and newState ('hidden' or 'visible').

definition: The reference element a floating element positions itself relative to. Also called the anchor element.
usage: Standardized by Anchor Intent (CSS Anchor Positioning anchor-name/position-anchor). The Anchored Element tethers to it.

definition: The floating element (tooltip, popover, menu) positioned relative to an anchor element. Maps to CSS Anchor Positioning's 'anchored element'.
usage: Standardized by Anchor Intent; positioned by a Positioning Provider via the Web Positioning plug.

definition: The paired entry-delay and minimum-hold contract that prevents a loading indicator from flashing on fast operations: the indicator is only shown once the wait passes an entry threshold (≈50ms), and once shown is held for a minimum duration (≈100ms) even if the operation resolves sooner. Both values are configurable defaults.
usage: Standardized by Loader Intent (the timing entry threshold + the holdFloor minimum hold).

definition: The preferred side and alignment of an anchored element relative to its anchor, on a 12-way grid (side × start/center/end) plus an automatic option.
usage: The placement dimension of the Anchor Intent. Mirrors CSS position-area and Floating UI placement.

definition: Detection of viewport or clipping-ancestor overflow, and the fallback response that keeps an anchored element visible.
usage: The collision dimension of the Anchor Intent: an ordered pipeline of flip/shift/hide/none.

definition: A collision strategy that moves the anchored element to the opposite side of the anchor when the preferred placement overflows.
usage: A value of the Anchor Intent collision pipeline. Maps to CSS position-try and Floating UI flip().

definition: The live binding that keeps an anchored element positioned relative to its anchor as the page scrolls, resizes, or mutates.
usage: Established by a Positioning Provider when tracking: 'follow'; returns a cleanup that detaches the tether.

definition: A registered engine that computes placement for anchored elements, satisfying the CustomPositioner contract (native CSS Anchor, Floating UI adapter, etc.).
usage: Registered in CustomPositioningRegistry and resolved through the injector chain. See Web Positioning.

definition: ARIA attribute indicating the current item within a set. For navigation, aria-current='page' identifies the current page link.
usage: Set by RouteLinkBehavior on the link matching the current URL. Screen readers announce it as 'current page'.

definition: ARIA attribute on a column (or row) header indicating the table's current sort state: ascending, descending, none (sortable but not currently sorted), or other (a non-standard order). Only one header is sorted at a time.
usage: The Data Table block projects the Collection Operations sortDirection onto it verbatim; the active sort key's <th> carries the direction, other sortable headers carry none.

definition: The WAI-ARIA APG recipe for a table whose columns can be reordered: a native <table> with <th scope="col"> headers, each sortable header wrapping its label in a <button>, and the active column carrying aria-sort. Distinct from role="grid", which adds cell-level keyboard navigation.
usage: The accessible-today pattern the Data Table block realizes for the sort stage of Collection Operations.

definition: How a set of active filter predicates combine: all (AND — a row must satisfy every predicate) or any (OR — a row satisfies at least one). The predicates themselves are call-site data, not part of the standard.
usage: The filterMatch dimension of the Collection Operations intent, realized by the Data Table block's filter stage.

definition: The aggregate value shown for each group when a collection is grouped — count, sum, average, minimum, or maximum — named after the SQL aggregate functions, the nearest cross-platform standard (the web has none).
usage: The groupSummary dimension of the Collection Operations intent; the Data Table block renders it in a scope="rowgroup" summary row per group.

definition: A reusable functionality pattern attached to a component via composition rather than inheritance.
usage: Implemented via Web Behaviors.

definition: The UX state where user interaction is prevented to ensure data integrity during a critical process.
usage: Defined in Loader Intent (Blocking Profile).

definition: A modular, visual UI component that follows strict interoperability standards.
usage: Standardized by Web Blocks.

definition: Viewport width thresholds that trigger layout changes. In Web Everything, these are tokenized to ensure consistency across JS and CSS.
usage: Standardized by Breakpoint Intent.

definition: A raw HTML file representing a specific usage scenario. Serves as the Single Source of Truth for documentation examples and E2E test fixtures.
usage: Located in `src/cases/`.

definition: Declarative trigger pattern from Invoker Commands API. Button references target by ID (commandfor) and specifies action (command). Custom commands use -- prefix.
usage: Standard pattern (Baseline Jan 2026). Example: <button commandfor="panel-1" command="--view-show">

definition: An encapsulated custom element with its own shadow DOM and lifecycle.

The convention where a <component> definition's own children are its render source, with no nested <template> wrapper required. A lone direct-child <template> remains the explicit, inert form; both lower to identical output.
usage: The <component> element's authoring default (DC-11). See the Component block.

definition: A custom element that participates in forms via static formAssociated + attachInternals() — gaining form/labels access, :disabled from a fieldset, and form-lifecycle callbacks.
usage: Declared on a <component> with the form-associated attribute (DC-12). Value & validation wiring composes with Web Validation.

definition: The accessibility role/state an element exposes by default via ElementInternals (internals.role, internals.aria*), overridable per-instance by a role= or aria-* attribute.
usage: Declared on a <component> with default-role="…" (DC-13). Borrows the platform's ElementInternals ARIAMixin vocabulary.

definition: Relocating a connected DOM node via Element.moveBefore() without disconnecting it, preserving live state (focus, media playback, iframes, shadow tree). Custom elements opt in by defining connectedMoveCallback.
usage: Declared on a <component> with the preserve-on-move attribute (DC-15). Mirrors the platform moveBefore()/connectedMoveCallback contract.

definition: A UI component managing focus for its children (e.g., Listbox, Grid) via specific keyboard patterns like Roving Tabindex.
usage: Standardized by Composite Widget Protocol.

definition: CSS property for lazy rendering. 'hidden' skips rendering but keeps element in accessibility tree. 'auto' renders only when near viewport.
usage: Standard (Baseline). Preferred for view:show as it provides best performance while maintaining accessibility.

definition: A mechanism for hierarchical data flow and service provision across component trees, independent of the DOM structure.
usage: Implemented by Web Contexts.

definition: A design pattern where a component's dependencies are provided to it, rather than created by it. Enables loose coupling and easy testing.
usage: Implemented by Web Injectors.

definition: Structural markers (loops, conditionals) and comment logic that manipulate the DOM structure.
usage: Implemented by Web Directives.

definition: The handling of text direction (LTR vs RTL) and its impact on layout, icons, and interaction logic.
usage: Core consideration for internationalization.

definition: A design pattern where a button controls the visibility of a content section. The button uses aria-expanded to communicate state.
usage: Implemented by NavSectionBehavior for collapsible navigation sections. Follows the W3C APG Disclosure Navigation pattern.

definition: The simulated Z-axis depth of an element, typically conveyed via shadows or layering, indicating hierarchy.
usage: Managed by Surface Intent.

definition: The strategy for forcing content into a container (Cover, Contain, Fill) preserving aspect ratio.
usage: Used across Media and Layout blocks.

definition: The orchestration of keyboard navigation and active element state. Includes trapping, restoring, and cycling focus.
usage: Managed by Interaction Intent.

definition: Error tolerance level where the operation failed but the application state remains valid.
usage: A core principle of Reliability Intent.

definition: Binary visibility states used in toggle events. Preferred over 'open/closed' or 'active/inactive' for consistency with Popover API.
usage: Standard event detail shape: { oldState: 'hidden', newState: 'visible' }

definition: HTML attribute value that hides content but allows browser search (Ctrl+F) to find and reveal it.
usage: Standard (Interop 2025). Excellent for accordions, FAQs, and searchable expandable content.

definition: Visual symbols standards including sizing scales, stroke weights, and positioning strategies to ensure optical balance.
usage: Standardized by Icon Intent.

definition: Conditional rendering that adds/removes content from DOM. Clean unmount with no state retention.
usage: Implemented by ViewIfDirective. Use for expensive components, auth gates, or when cleanup is needed. Contrast with view:show (ViewShowBehavior) which toggles visibility only.

definition: HTML attribute that makes element and descendants non-interactive. Removed from tab order and accessibility tree for interaction.
usage: Standard. Use for preview states, disabled sections, or content behind modals.

definition: Standardization of data entry interfaces, including affordances, privacy masking, virtual keyboard types, and visual status rendering. Status renders the DisplayDecision from Validation Intent (see OP-21).
usage: Standardized by Input Intent. The status dimension visually presents validity state from Validation Intent; there is one source of truth for field state.

definition: The handling of input modalities (pointer, touch, gamepad, keyboard) and the feedback loops required for each.
usage: Standardized by Interaction Intent.

definition: Feature that preserves component state when hidden instead of destroying it. Supports max cache limit with LRU eviction.
usage: Inspired by Vue KeepAlive. Essential for tabs where users expect form state to persist.

definition: A system for registering and handling key combinations that works with Shadow DOM and standard OS conventions.
usage: Standardized by Web Blocks.

definition: The organization of protocols from abstract intents down to concrete implementation details.
usage: Core philosophy of Web Everything.

definition: The application shell structure and region mechanics (Drawers, Rails, Panels) that define the macro-layout.
usage: Standardized by Layout Intent.

definition: The standardized state machine (Idle -> Loading -> Success/Error) that drives the UI. It separates the 'Process' (Time) from the 'Outcome' (Data).
usage: Resource Loaders and Actions must expose their lifecycle capabilities.

definition: The visualization of pending operations, defining how the UI reacts (Blocking, Skeleton, Spinner) to latency.
usage: Standardized by Loader Intent.

definition: The system for prioritizing and displaying communication to the user, from subtle toasts to blocking alerts.
usage: Standardized by Message Intent.

definition: A dialog or overlay that forces user interaction before returning to the main content. Defines stacking, scrolling, and dismissal behavior.
usage: Standardized by Modal Intent.

definition: A self-contained unit of functionality or UI that expects a specific host context.
usage: Building block of Web Architecture.

definition: The physics, latency tolerance, and transition behaviors that define the 'feel' of the application.
usage: Standardized by Motion Intent.

definition: Attribute that groups elements for mutual exclusivity. Only one element with the same name value can be active/visible at a time.
usage: Standard pattern from HTML (radio buttons, <details name> accordion). Preferred over 'group' attribute.

definition: The structure of application flow (Hierarchical, Lateral, Linear) and the mechanism for transitioning between views.
usage: Standardized by Navigation Intent.

definition: A nav element that identifies a section containing navigation links. Use aria-label when multiple landmarks exist on the same page.
usage: NavListBehavior attaches to nav elements. The HTML nav element creates an implicit navigation ARIA landmark.

definition: A named insertion point for routed or projected content. Enables router integration and parent-controlled content projection.
usage: Term from React Router and Angular. Use view:outlet="name" for named outlets.

definition: Code that implements a feature on web browsers that do not support the feature.
usage: Provided by Web Plugs.

definition: Declarative strategies for placing elements relative to the viewport or other elements.
usage: Standardized by Layout (regions) and the Anchor Intent (tethered surfaces). Element-relative anchoring is delegated to a Positioning Provider via the Web Positioning plug.

definition: The strategy of loading resources before they are required, balanced against bandwidth and battery constraints.
usage: Standardized by Prefetch Intent.

definition: A system for defining and looking up capabilities or components via a global or scoped lookup mechanism.
usage: Implemented by Web Registries.

definition: Strategies for error recovery, retry policies, and maintaining a viable state during failure conditions.
usage: Standardized by Reliability Intent.

definition: A behavioral directive that attaches asynchronous mutation lifecycle management to UI triggers.
usage: Part of Web Blocks.

definition: A structural pattern used to handle asynchronous data fetching and state display in-place.
usage: Part of Web Blocks.

definition: A focus management pattern where `tabindex="0"` moves to the currently active item, while others are set to `-1`.
usage: Used in Composite Widgets. One of the two strategies of the Focus Delegation Intent (the other being Virtual Focus).

definition: A focus management pattern where DOM focus stays on a controller element (e.g. a text input or listbox) while the visually active item is tracked via `aria-activedescendant`. Unlike real focus, the active item must be scrolled into view manually — browsers do not auto-scroll `aria-activedescendant` targets.
usage: The `virtual` strategy of the Focus Delegation Intent; required for comboboxes where typing must continue uninterrupted. Complements Roving Tabindex.

definition: A pattern where moving focus to an item also activates or selects it (single-select listboxes, auto-activated tabs), as opposed to focus moving independently of selection (multi-select, menus, comboboxes), where selection commits on an explicit key.
usage: The `selectionFollowsFocus` dimension of the Focus Delegation Intent; composes with the Selection Intent's commitment model.

definition: The behavior of choosing items from a set, defining cardinality (Single vs Multi) and required state.
usage: Standardized by Selection Intent.

definition: A placeholder shaped like the content that will (or may) load, shown during an indeterminate wait. It sets the user's expectation of the incoming content's nature and limits layout shift when real content arrives; preferably subtly animated to indicate ongoing work. Distinct from a loader (an abstract activity animation such as a spinner) in that it mirrors the content's shape.
usage: Standardized by Loader Intent (the skeleton value of the indicator dimension, typically over the replacement strategy).

definition: Toggle visibility without removing from DOM. Element remains in accessibility tree and preserves state.
usage: Implemented by ViewShowBehavior (view:show) for expression-bound visibility, or ViewBehavior (view attr) for command-driven toggling. Use for frequent toggles where state preservation matters.

definition: Standard primitives for observability, data binding, and reaction to data changes (Signals, Stores).
usage: Implemented by Web States.

definition: The definition of visual depth (Z-Axis), material qualities (Opaque, Transparent), and their interactive properties.
usage: Standardized by Surface Intent.

definition: Multi-branch conditional rendering where one case is shown based on expression value. More declarative than multiple if statements.
usage: Implemented by ViewSwitchDirective. Matches Angular ngSwitch, Solid Switch/Match. Use view:switch on parent, view:case/view:default on children.

definition: ARIA roles for accessible tab interfaces. Container has role=tablist, triggers have role=tab, content has role=tabpanel.
usage: Required by WAI-ARIA Tabs Pattern. Must include aria-selected, aria-controls, aria-labelledby.

definition: The unified handling of temporal selection and display across different input modalities (Calendar, Dial, Text).
usage: Standardized by Temporal Intent.

definition: Event fired after a visibility change completes. Non-cancelable notification of completed state transition.
usage: Matches Popover API and <details> element pattern. Event detail contains oldState and newState.

definition: A parsed unit of meaning within a text node or attribute, often used for data binding.
usage: Handled by Web Tokens.

definition: A record of an execution path through the application. Composed of multiple 'Spans' (units of work).
usage: Standardized by Web Traces.

definition: An engine responsible for recording execution flow (Traces) and context. It can be injected into any scope.
usage: Standardized by Web Traces.

definition: A categorization tag for traces (e.g., 'Network', 'Render', 'UserInteraction') allowing different tracers to subscribe to specific domains.
usage: Standardized by Web Traces.

definition: A granular, composable capability mixed into a component class (e.g., `withSortable`).
usage: Standardized by Web Traits.

definition: An element that initiates an action on another element. In tabs: tab-trigger. In dropdowns: dropdown-trigger.
usage: Common term across UI libraries. With Invoker Commands, triggers use commandfor attribute.

definition: A keyboard navigation pattern where typed characters accumulate into a buffer that moves focus to the first item whose label matches; the buffer resets after an idle timeout. Distinct from filtering — items are never removed.
usage: Standardized by Type-Ahead Intent and the Type-Ahead block. Consumed by Droplist and composes with Composite Widget.

definition: The abstraction of visual text hierarchy (Headline, Body, Caption) from semantic HTML elements.
usage: Standardized by Typography Intent.

definition: The lifecycle of data integrity checking, error message placement, and submission blocking.
usage: Standardized by Validation Intent.

definition: A region of content whose visibility is managed. Framework-agnostic term covering show/hide, structural rendering, and command-driven toggling.
usage: Foundation module providing ViewEngine (shared core), ViewBehavior (view attr), ViewShowBehavior (view:show), ViewIfDirective (view:if), ViewSwitchDirective (view:switch). Higher-level components (Tabs, Router) build on these.

definition: Shared utility providing hidden mode management, toggle events (beforetoggle/toggle), exclusive group coordination, and View Transitions integration.
usage: Not a block itself. Imported by View blocks and higher-level components (Router uses it directly). Handles HOW to hide/show; blocks handle WHEN.

definition: An element implementation using markers (e.g., comments) to avoid introducing redundant container nodes in the DOM.
usage: Used elsewhere to preserve CSS Grid/Flex relationships.

definition: See 'Case'.
usage: See 'Case'.

definition: A URL pattern to view mapping. Defines which content displays for a given URL path.
usage: Standard term from all major routers. Pattern syntax follows URLPattern API: /users/:id, /files/*.

definition: Conditional navigation logic that can allow, prevent, or redirect navigation. Common types: canActivate, canDeactivate, canLoad.
usage: Term from Angular Router. Used as route:guard="requireAuth" for entry guards, route:guard:leave="confirmUnsaved" for exit guards.

definition: Async function that fetches data before a route activates. Result available in route context.
usage: Term from React Router 6+ and Remix. Used as route:loader="loadUser". Data accessible via @route.data.

definition: Modern browser API (Baseline 2024) replacing History API. Provides navigation events, traversal info, and interception capabilities.
usage: Primary navigation mechanism. Use navigation.addEventListener('navigate', ...) for route interception.

definition: Web standard API (Baseline 2025) for matching URLs against patterns with named groups and wildcards.
usage: Use for route pattern matching. Syntax: /users/:id for named params, * for wildcards, :param? for optional.

definition: Strategy for loading route resources before navigation. Can load on hover, when visible, or eagerly.
usage: Use route:prefetch="hover" on links. Integrates with Speculation Rules API when available.

definition: Browser or custom control over scroll position after navigation. Navigation API provides native support.
usage: Configure via route:config scroll="auto|manual|none". 'auto' uses browser behavior, 'manual' for custom control.

definition: The specification of what server output MUST look like for a given Web Everything primitive, and what the client expects during hydration.
usage: Each project defines its SSR Contract in a dedicated section on its project page.

definition: The process by which client-side JavaScript attaches interactivity to server-rendered HTML without re-rendering the DOM.
usage: Described in each project's SSR Contract section.

definition: The minimum functionality available when JavaScript is disabled or not yet loaded. Server-rendered content should be usable at this baseline.
usage: Defined per project in SSR Contracts.

definition: A DOM annotation (comment node, data attribute, or JSON script block) that enables the client to associate server-rendered content with its reactive bindings.
usage: Used by Web Expressions SSR for expression rebinding. Formats: comment markers, data-expr attributes, JSON hydration maps.

definition: The <template shadowrootmode> pattern that allows shadow DOM content to be included in server-rendered HTML and parsed by the browser without JavaScript.
usage: Used by Web Components SSR Contract for custom elements with shadow DOM.

definition: The tier at which a validation implementation conforms to the Validation Intent contract. Level 0: Intent-aware. Level 1: observable states and events (swappable). Level 2: canonical shapes and DI concerns (combinable).
usage: Standardized by Validation Intent. Conformance is defined against observable contract, never computation strategy.

definition: The four parallel regions of a validation control's state: Validity (merged + version token), Interaction (dirty/touched/focused/submitted), Commit (committed/rejected/held), Display (silent/showing-{severity}).
usage: Defined in Validation Intent per-level vocabulary. Each region is independently observable and transitions emit events.

definition: An async validation state carrying a version/generation token. The token is the input's snapshot version at dispatch time; results from old versions are discarded (stale-resolve prevention).
usage: Composes Loader Intent's pending lifecycle. Cross-cutting requirement for all async validation systems (RHF #1688, Final Form #420).

definition: The validation-level dimension that defines data persistence rules: error rejects (blocks submission), warning/info commit, caution holds until user confirms.
usage: Standardized by Validation Intent level dimension. Separates severity/tone from data-gating behavior.

definition: A form-level aggregate list of all validation errors, displayed at the top (GOV.UK pattern). Ordered by DOM, linked to fields, focused on submit-blocked, with verbatim parity to inline messages.
usage: Optional feature validation.feature.error-summary at Conformance Level 1+. Combines with inline messages for dual-model a11y and discoverability.

definition: A display policy: until a field errors, show validation lazily (on blur). Once errored, flip to eager (on change) for immediate feedback. Revert to lazy on fix. A dynamic behavior (not a flat execution value) driven by interaction state.
usage: Emerges naturally from Validation Intent's DisplayDecision gating by visibility intent. Research: Konjević, Smashing Magazine. Prevents annoying re-validation while encouraging rapid correction.

definition: The concern that determines whether current field value differs from its baseline (pristine state). Algorithms: strict ===, deep equal, normalized (trim/case), custom comparators.
usage: A decomposed DI concern (customValidation:dirty-check). Swappable per control or app-wide. Required for interaction state tracking.

definition: The concern that detects when a field value has changed: event listeners, signal subscriptions, polling, MutationObserver. Triggers value-input events.
usage: A decomposed DI concern (customValidation:change-detection). The event-driven vs state-derived architectural choice collapses into this concern + inter-element-comms; the other 6 concerns are architecture-agnostic.

definition: The unified semantic axis shared by Validation (level), Feedback (severity), and Message (tone): error (failure), caution (critical advisory), warning (advisory), info (informational), success (confirmation).
usage: Defined across intents. Allows validation messages, toasts, and alerts to speak the same language. Replaces the triplicated vocabulary (feedback.severity / message.tone / validation.level).

definition: A dependsOn relation at group scope: field B depends on field A. When A changes, B is revalidated reactively. Enables cross-field and conditional validation without imperative handlers.
usage: Optional feature validation.feature.cross-field. Required for password-confirm, date ranges, required-if patterns. Fixes RHF #trigger and Angular updateValueAndValidity revalidation complexity.

definition: A service registered in CustomRecoveryHandlerRegistry that decides whether and how to recover from a mechanism failure. Implements tryRecover(error, context) returning a RecoveryResult or null to decline. Handlers are tried in registration order; the first non-null result wins.
usage: Web Reliability. Distinct from validation error handling — recovery handlers respond to system/operation failures (timeouts, server errors), not input invalidity.

definition: The instruction a recovery handler returns to the consumer: retry (re-execute after optional delay), queued (stored for later replay), fallback (surface fallback content), or abort (propagate error immediately).
usage: Web Reliability — RecoveryResult.outcome. Maps to Reliability Intent dimensions: retry → recovery.automatic/manual; fallback → fallback.skeleton/empty/previous; abort → tolerance.terminal.

definition: An unexpected system or operation error — network unreachable, server crash, database timeout, computation failure. Distinguished from input invalidity (Validation domain): the user's data was correct; the mechanism broke.
usage: Web Reliability, Reliability Intent. Determines which domain owns the error: mechanism failures → Reliability; input errors → Validation.

definition: The observability concern of detecting, representing, and attributing mutations to state over time. A facet of observability in general — not limited to form dirty-state — that recurs across stores, signals, documents, collections, the DOM, and network sync, and that the field solves with plural strategies (snapshot diff, Proxy, signals, patches, CRDTs, event sourcing).
usage: The framing term for the Change Tracking standard (research topic change-tracking-observability). Generalizes the validation customValidation:dirty-check and customValidation:change-detection concerns. Goal: configurable preferred strategy + interop between multiple strategies in one app.

definition: The normalized representation of a single mutation that every change-tracking strategy maps into: path (RFC 6901 pointer), op (RFC 6902 vocabulary), optional oldValue/newValue, source (attribution), timestamp, and optional version causal token. A lossy lingua franca for observation — not a replacement for any strategy's native form.
usage: Provisional interop target (research topic change-tracking-observability); shape to be finalized in the Change Tracking protocol. oldValue is carried because RFC 6902 ops are not self-inverting.

definition: The origin of a mutation carried on a Change Record so cross-layer consumers can discriminate cause: user input, program mutation, server-sync, replay/undo, or external. May carry a W3C Trace Context traceparent linking the change to the initiating span.
usage: The attribution axis of Change Tracking. Generalizes validation's value-input (user) vs baseline-change (program/server) distinction; mirrors the CloudEvents source envelope field.

definition: A registered mechanism that turns a tracked target into a stream of Change Records, satisfying the CustomChangeStrategy contract (track, optional diff, optional applyInverse). Capability is feature-detected by which optional methods are present. Examples: native-signals, snapshot-diff, proxy, patch, crdt, event-sourced.
usage: Change Tracking Protocol (Web States). The native store/signal subscription strategy is the zero-config default; library strategies are opt-in adapters behind the same contract.

definition: The CustomChangeStrategyRegistry resolved through the injector chain that selects the active Change Strategy per scope, so different subtrees of one app can track changes with different strategies simultaneously (e.g. a form on snapshot-diff, a collaborative document on a CRDT).
usage: Change Tracking Protocol (Web States). Generalizes the validation customValidation:change-detection concern from one form to all observable state. Nearest-scope strategy wins.

definition: A registered mechanism that turns a parsed element tree plus a change source into committed DOM updates, satisfying the CustomRenderStrategy contract (mount, optional update, optional dispose). Capability is feature-detected by which optional methods are present — a strategy without update is mount-once. The four registered strategies are declarative-static (the native-first default), vdom, fine-grained, and imperative. The sibling of Change Strategy: change strategies detect what mutated, render strategies commit how the DOM reflects it.
usage: Render Strategy Protocol (Web Components). A technical contract, not a UX preference — surfaces under /protocols/, never /intents/. The JSX adapter and the declarative <component> element are consumers that target a chosen strategy.

definition: The CustomRenderStrategyRegistry resolved through the injector chain that selects the active Render Strategy per scope, so different subtrees of one app render with different strategies simultaneously (a static header on declarative-static, a live grid on fine-grained). Mirrors the resolution rule of Change Strategy Registry — nearest-scope wins.
usage: Render Strategy Protocol (Web Components). The registry is open, not a fixed enum: a future platform target (DOM Parts / Template Instantiation) registers as another provider without touching the contract.

definition: Compiling a higher-level render-strategy expression of control-flow into a lower-level declarative-static primitive — e.g. JS items.map(…) into <template is="for-each">, or a ternary into <template is="if">. The inverse of Lifting. Structural control-flow lowers reversibly; the eager {expr} ⇄ reactive {{ expr }} boundary is lossy.
usage: Render Strategy Protocol cross-strategy compiler. Distinct from same-strategy spelling conversion (Axis-1), which is reversible by construction.

definition: The inverse of Lowering — recovering a higher-level render-strategy expression (JS items.map(…)) from a declarative-static directive (<template is="for-each">). Structural directives lift cleanly; lifting cannot recover whether a {{ }} path was authored as an eager value or a tracked binding, so that boundary is declared lossy.
usage: Render Strategy Protocol cross-strategy compiler.

definition: A thin bridge that maps a third-party change source (Immer/Mutative patches, Yjs/Automerge ops, a JSON Patch stream) into the canonical Change Record contract, or between two strategies' formats. Because mappings are lossy, bridges are adapters rather than identities.
usage: Change Tracking Protocol (Web States). Enables a signals graph → JSON-Patch stream → CRDT document pipeline with the Change Record as the hand-off.

definition: The pristine reference snapshot a diff or dirty strategy compares the current value against to derive change. Re-captured on a baseline-set event (initial load, save, server sync). For SSR, the serialized initial store state is the post-hydration baseline.
usage: Change Tracking Protocol (Web States); reconciles with the Validation Intent's existing baseline notion behind customValidation:dirty-check.

definition: Change detection by comparing two immutable snapshots (baseline vs current) and deriving the set of Change Records between them. Sampled coverage — intermediate states between snapshots are invisible — but library-agnostic and trivially correct; the natural fallback when no interception is available.
usage: A Change Strategy supplying the optional diff(baseline, current) method. Contrast with interception strategies (Proxy, signals) that capture every write as it happens.

definition: A serializable, optionally invertible set of Change Records aligned to JSON Patch (RFC 6902). Forward patches apply a change; inverse patches (carrying oldValue, since RFC 6902 ops are not self-inverting) revert it, enabling undo/replay. Immer and Mutative emit forward + inverse patch pairs.
usage: The transport-friendly representation produced by patch and snapshot Change Strategies. The change-reverted event applies an inverse Change Patch.

definition: Turning a single UI into many locales: composing messages from static text plus formatted values, resolving keys against catalogs, walking locale fallback chains, and adapting writing direction. Distinct from native value formatting, which the platform already provides via Intl.
usage: The domain of Web Intl, specified by the Localization Protocol and declared by the Locale Intent.

definition: Expanding a requested locale into an ordered fallback chain of available locales (e.g. fr-CA → fr → en), so a missing translation degrades gracefully to a more general locale rather than failing.
usage: Performed by the LocaleNegotiation interface; the Localization Protocol walks the resulting chain when resolving a key.

definition: The ordered list of locales tried in turn when resolving a message key, produced by Locale Negotiation. Resolution stops at the first locale for which a Translation Provider returns a message.
usage: Walked by the Localization Protocol's resolution pattern; the outermost fallback is typically the application's default locale.

definition: A keyed set of message sources for one locale (and optionally one namespace). The data a Translation Provider reads to resolve a key into a Message Function.
usage: Loaded eagerly or lazily by a Translation Provider. Authored as JSON / PO / XLIFF / ARB / Fluent and normalized at build time.

definition: The Unicode message-formatting syntax (UTS #35 Part 9, published 2024) for composing messages with variable interpolation and plural/gender selection. Forward-compatible with the TC39 Intl.MessageFormat proposal.
usage: The default message syntax of the Localization Protocol; compiled by a MessageFormatter into a Message Function. Abbreviated MF2.

definition: A resolved message represented as (params?) => string — a function that formats a message with the given parameters using native Intl. The common shape for runtime (MF2) and compile-time (tree-shaken) messages alike.
usage: Returned by CustomTranslationProvider.resolve(); typed as MessageFn.

definition: A registered source that resolves a message key for a locale to a Message Function, or declines with null. A native Intl MF2 formatter, lazy-namespace catalog, or library adapter (FormatJS, i18next, Lingui, Paraglide).
usage: Satisfies the CustomTranslationProvider contract; registered in CustomTranslationProviderRegistry and resolved through the injector chain. First provider that resolves a key owns the message.

definition: Bidirectional text handling — laying out content that mixes left-to-right and right-to-left scripts per the Unicode bidirectional algorithm. The reason layout flows by inline start/end rather than physical left/right.
usage: Driven by the Locale Intent direction dimension via the dir attribute, :dir(), and CSS logical properties. direction: auto defers to the bidi algorithm.

definition: Changing the application's active locale at runtime, and the delivery strategy governing how the change reaches the UI: synchronously from preloaded catalogs (instant), via an on-demand catalog fetch behind a loading state (async), or through a full per-locale document load (reload).
usage: The switching dimension of the Translation Intent. Orthogonal to which locale is active (the Locale Intent). async composes with the Loader Intent; reload with URL-based locale routing.

definition: A DOM region whose updates are announced to assistive technology without moving focus, via `aria-live` (`polite`/`assertive`) or an implicit role (`status`, `alert`, `log`). The region must already exist (empty) when the page loads so the AT registers it; text is then written into it on events.
usage: Standardized by the Live Region Status Intent. Announces loading, result counts, no-results, and errors. Distinct from the Message/Feedback intents, which own notification content and priority.

definition: Rendering only the visible window of a large collection (plus a small overscan) and recycling rows as the user scrolls, rather than placing every item in the DOM. Also called virtualization. Distinct from the Virtual Element (comment-marker) concept.
usage: The `windowed` rendering mode of the Windowed Collection Intent. Requires Set Size / Position in Set to preserve accessibility, and must not unmount the focused item.

definition: The `aria-setsize` (total item count) and `aria-posinset` (1-based absolute index) attributes that let assistive technology announce an item's position in a collection — e.g. 'item 42 of 5000' — even when not all items are present in the DOM.
usage: Required on every rendered item under Windowing; the index is the item's absolute position in the full collection, never its DOM order.

definition: Visually cutting text that overflows its box (`clip`, `ellipsis`, or `fade`) while keeping the full string as the element's text content. Must not slice mid-grapheme, and must provide a recovery path (tooltip/title or expand) to read the full value.
usage: The `truncation` dimension of the Typography Intent. Preserves WCAG 2.5.3 Label in Name because the accessible name stays the full text; front-load distinguishing words so they survive the cut.

definition: An 'X' affordance that resets a field's value. Shown only when a value is present, carries the accessible name 'Clear', is keyboard-reachable, and returns focus to the field after clearing. Decoupled from Escape so clearing is a deliberate action, not an accidental side-effect of dismissing a popup.
usage: The `clear` affordance of the Input Intent. Reused by search fields, comboboxes, tag inputs, and filters.

definition: A labeled subset of options within a selection control. Native: `<optgroup label>` (label required, no nesting). Custom: `role="group"` + `aria-labelledby` referencing a visible heading; the heading is not a focusable option.
usage: The `grouping` dimension of the Selection Intent. Mirrors how menus, command palettes, and faceted lists organize choices.

definition: A custom element whose definition — tag name, template, and shadow options — is expressed entirely in HTML markup, requiring no authored JavaScript class.
usage: The output of the <component> element in Web Components. Distinct from Declarative Shadow DOM, which declares an instance's shadow content but not a reusable element definition.

definition: The <component name> declaration block — a name, a <template>, and shadow/registration options — that registers a Declarative Custom Element.
usage: Authored once per element type. The deterministic build-time AST transform lowers each Component Definition to a class plus customElements.define().

definition: The <component> element itself: a transient authoring element that reads a Component Definition, registers the custom element, then removes itself from the DOM leaving no wrapper node.
usage: Web Components. Shares the self-removal lifecycle of a Transient Component, but its purpose is registration rather than rendering.

definition: The act of choosing which traits to compose and what dimension values they take when constructing a component. Local, total, and developer-owned — expressed as attributes on the substrate (e.g. selection="model=single") or as a list of with* factories in TypeScript. Distinct from Ambient Intent, which is the designer-owned behavioral-preference channel propagated via DI.
usage: The structural-construction channel of the Droplist family. Structural dimensions (model, editable) never travel via Ambient Intent — they are always stated locally. Behavioral dimensions (focusStrategy, collision) may fall back to Ambient Intent when not stated.

definition: A designer- or product-owned behavioral preference (focus strategy, collision handling, truncation) propagated down a DOM subtree via DI. Partial, overridable, and never structural — Ambient Intent cannot set model or editable; those are Trait Selection.
usage: The behavioral-preference channel in the Droplist family and other composable systems. Set via injector.set('customContexts:droplistIntent', { focusStrategy: 'virtual', … }); resolved per-dimension as explicit ⊕ ambient ⊕ default. The Loader Intent's loaderIntent provider is the original precedent.

definition: The abstract family — the substrate that every list-based selection surface (Dropdown, Multi-dropdown, Autocomplete, Tree-select, Menu button, native <select>) conforms to. Not itself a shippable widget.
usage: Standardized by the Droplist block. Documents the trait surface map, the two-channel resolution rule, and the preset table that each concrete member resolves into.

definition: One concrete member of the Droplist family — single-select, button-triggered, non-filtering. Structural identity: selection="model=single" with type-ahead.
usage: Standardized by the Dropdown block. A form-associated custom element so <form>/FormData/reset/validity work like the native <select> baseline.

definition: What a navigation contributes to session history: push (new entry), replace (overwrite current), or none (no entry). Determines what the browser Back button does after the navigation.
usage: The history dimension of the Navigation Intent. Maps to the Navigation API navigate(url, { history }) option.

definition: A URL that encodes enough application state (path + search params) to reconstruct a specific view directly — bookmarkable, shareable, and surviving a page refresh.
usage: The url value of the Navigation Intent's persistence dimension. Contrast memory (ephemeral, in-memory state) and session (carried in History state, survives traversal but not a fresh share).

definition: An animated transition played as one view replaces another during navigation, driven by the View Transitions API. Its direction (forward vs back) is a navigation concern; its feel (easing, reduced-motion) is a motion concern.
usage: The transition dimension of the Navigation Intent owns direction (directional|fade|none); the Motion intent's physics owns feel. Browser-initiated Back navigations carry no transition type, so direction is derived from history-traversal direction.

definition: Logic that can interrupt a navigation away from the current view — proceeding freely, prompting for confirmation (e.g. unsaved changes), or blocking until a condition clears.
usage: The guard dimension of the Navigation Intent (free|confirm|blocked). Grounded in the Navigation API navigate event (preventDefault/intercept) and beforeunload. Distinct from a route entry guard (canActivate); a leave guard is canDeactivate. The Router exposes it as route:guard:leave.

definition: A zero-height marker element placed at the boundary of a scrollable list. When it enters the viewport, an IntersectionObserver fires and the next slice is requested — the native-first grounding for auto-advance (infinite scroll) without scroll-position math.
usage: The Pagination block wires advance:auto (the Collection Operations advance dimension) to a sentinel observed by IntersectionObserver. Rendering of the grown list delegates to Windowed Collection; the loadingMore lifecycle to Loader. Only meaningful with pageMode:append (append + auto = infinite scroll).

definition: A status label that names the visible slice of a paged collection — e.g. ‘showing 21–40 of 500’. Requires a known total, so it composes only over an offset/page resource protocol; a cursor protocol has no total and forces it off.
usage: The rangeLabel dimension of the Collection Operations Intent, surfaced by the Pagination block's withRangeLabel trait. Recommended page-size options, defaults, and how the label degrades when the total is unavailable are unverified — owned by backlog #060.

definition: A user interaction that mutates the order of items in a collection while leaving the item set unchanged. Distinct from a computed sort (which derives order from a comparator) and from data transfer (which moves a payload between zones).
usage: Standardized by the Reorder Intent and realized by the Reorderable List block. The manual counterpart to the Collection Operations sort dimension — a list is hand-ordered or sorted, never both at once.

definition: A list whose item order is user-mutable by both pointer drag and keyboard, with live-region announcement and a pluggable commit strategy. Relocates items atomically with Element.moveBefore() so a moved item keeps its focus, animation, and connection state.
usage: The Reorderable List block; implements the Reorder Intent. Pairs each item with preserve-on-move for the atomic relocation.

definition: A scoped affordance within an item that initiates a drag, as opposed to the whole item being draggable. Leaves the rest of the item interactive (links, buttons) while still allowing it to be moved.
usage: The handle value of the Reorder Intent's grab dimension, surfaced by the Reorderable List block's withDragHandle trait. Marked in markup with [data-reorder-handle].

definition: The keyboard-operable path for reordering — roving tabindex over items plus grab/move/drop keys plus a polite live-region announcement of each move. It is a mandatory part of the reorder contract, not an enhancement; the deprecated aria-grabbed/aria-dropeffect are not used.
usage: The keyboard value of the Reorder Intent's movement dimension, surfaced by the Reorderable List block's withKeyboardReorder trait. Composes Focus Delegation and Live Region Status.

definition: The strategy that decides where a new order is written after a reorder — ephemeral (session only), localStorage, or written back to the source. Distinct from the validation Commit Policy, which governs when form field edits are written.
usage: The commit dimension of the Reorder Intent, surfaced by the Reorderable List block's withCommitStrategy trait behind the cancelable reorder-commit event. Defaults to ephemeral.
